old_claim.note: Electricity provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Continuous current from a Voltaic pile was the constitutive mechanism used to decompose water and other solutions.

source_shape.source_locator: The Philosophical Magazine, volume 7, pp. 337-347, especially pp. 338-339: Nicholson and Carlisle break a Voltaic-pile circuit with water between two conductors and observe hydrogen at one wire and oxygen at the other.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The founding experiment decomposed water by passing continuous electrical current from a Voltaic pile through it.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Electrical current is the mechanism that defines electrolysis; without it the scoped process is ordinary chemistry rather than electrochemistry.

ontology_before: Electricity was treated as one optional enabling path for a node whose mechanism is electrical decomposition.
ontology_after: Electricity is a required direct prerequisite of the 1800 Voltaic-electrolysis milestone.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-16-voltaic-electrolysis-scope: electricity remains direct and is typed required.
would_reject_if: The node is rescoped away from electrolysis to a non-electrical chemical process.
short_reason: Continuous electric current is constitutive of electrolysis.
